1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and versioning of the document.
   Expected Content: Title, version history, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Data Integration Principles Document
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected Content: Summary of the document's purpose, key objectives, and importance in the context of data governance.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the foundational data integration principles and scope necessary for effective data governance within the DII domain.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance framework.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Integration and Interoperability (DII)
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data landscape and integration needs.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being undertaken.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DII-01 - Define foundational data integration principles and scope
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish principles for data integration to ensure consistency and governance alignment.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Core source and target systems involved in data integration.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define data integration principles and scope boundaries.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of core source and target systems.
   5.3 Deliverables: Data integration principles document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Identify Core Systems
   - Draft Initial Principles
   - Align with Governance Expectations
   - Incorporate Lifecycle Checkpoints
   - Validate Against DCAM Scoping
   - Finalize Documentation
   - Obtain Approval
   - Communicate and Train

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the governance framework supporting the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in data integration.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: Core source and target systems identified.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Accountability, stewardship, ownership.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council as the primary authority.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner responsible for data domain decisions.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Stakeholder interviews for system identification.
   - Drafting and reviewing principles.
   - Validation against governance expectations.
   - Final approval and communication.
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Governance Office.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Non-core systems not included in the integration scope.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the management and ownership of the document.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office responsible for maintenance.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Data integration principles template.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA DMBOK2 guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Preliminary report on core systems.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.

   5.3 Deliverables: Integration architecture overview v0.1.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Define Integration Principles
   - Draft Integration Architecture Overview
   - Align with Metadata Structures
   - Incorporate Data Quality Controls
   - Define Stewardship and Ownership Responsibilities
   - Iterate Using CMMI Practices
   - Establish Governance Checkpoints
   - Finalize Integration Architecture Overview

   Purpose: To delineate the governance framework supporting the action.

   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets relevant to DII.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, integration, stewardship.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Roles of Data Owner, Data Steward, and Data Custodian.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined by the Data Governance Council.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Document foundational principles
   - Review integration architecture drafts
   - Approve final architecture overview
   - Monitor compliance with governance policies
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Security governance.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes implementation details and operational procedures.
